name: Avocado
summary: A fatty fruit whose monounsaturated fat improves cholesterol, plus more potassium per gram than a banana.
superfoodReason: A rare fruit rich in heart-healthy monounsaturated fat, fiber, and potassium, with randomized-trial evidence for improving LDL cholesterol.

benefits.mechanism: Avocado's monounsaturated fat replaces saturated fat in the diet, lowering LDL cholesterol, and its antioxidants reduce oxidation of small, dense LDL particles — the more atherogenic kind. A randomized controlled trial of one avocado a day showed both effects.

citations.title: A moderate-fat diet with one avocado per day increases plasma antioxidants and decreases the oxidation of small, dense LDL: a randomized controlled trial (Wang et al., J Nutr 2020)

benefits.claim: Rich in potassium for blood pressure
benefits.mechanism: Potassium counteracts sodium and relaxes blood-vessel walls, helping lower blood pressure. Avocado is one of the most potassium-dense fruits — gram for gram, more than a banana.

citations.title: Potassium — NIH Office of Dietary Supplements (Health Professional Fact Sheet)

surprisingFacts.text: Botanically a single-seeded berry, the avocado has more potassium per 100g than a banana — despite the banana's reputation as the potassium fruit.
